# Test that drive line intersection vertices land exactly on graph edges,
# regardless of whether the drive line endpoint is before or after the edge.

polygon outer
0,0
300,0
300,200
0,200
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

generate
----
total_stalls: 121

# Dump the auto graph to identify the vertical edge we'll cross.
# At 90° default angle, interior aisles run vertically.
# Vertical edge at x≈180.3 between vertices 7 (180.3,39.9) and 8 (180.3,160.1).
graph
----
0: 28,28
1: 272,28
2: 272,172
3: 28,172
4: 230,122
5: 520,122
6: 375,412
7: 216,28
8: 216,172
9: 160,28
10: 160,172
11: 105,28
12: 105,172

# Short drive line: endpoint BEFORE the vertical edge at x≈180.
# Must project outward → t_end captures the crossing.
# Vertex 10 should land exactly on the vertical edge at x≈180.3.
drive-line
50,100
130,100
----
drive-line: 50,100 -> 130,100

graph precise
----
0: 27.9,27.9
1: 272.1,27.9
2: 272.1,172.1
3: 27.9,172.1
4: 229.8,122.1
5: 520.2,122.1
6: 375.0,412.4
7: 216.3,27.9
8: 216.3,172.1
9: 160.5,27.9
10: 160.5,172.1
11: 104.6,27.9
12: 104.6,172.1
13: 27.9,100.0
14: 104.6,100.0
15: 160.5,100.0

screenshot drive-line-intersection-short.png
----
